steps.title: Define what the goal requires
steps.content: The student wants to "compare some disadvantages" of docked and dockless programs. The notes list one disadvantage for each: docked programs require significant space and money; dockless programs can be disorganized. A correct answer must reference a real disadvantage of each type — not advantages, and not misrepresent the notes.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Dockless programs can be disorganized; docked programs, on the other hand, offer order and consistency.': Mentions one dockless disadvantage but pairs it with a docked advantage — not a comparison of disadvantages. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Worth noting is that while dockless programs are relatively easy and inexpensive to implement, they are less flexible than docked programs.': Highlights a dockless advantage (easy/inexpensive) and then misrepresents the notes — the notes say dockless offers more flexibility, not less. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Docked programs are more resource-intensive than dockless programs, but they avoid some of the latter's organizational challenges.': Names the docked disadvantage (resource-intensive) and the dockless disadvantage (organizational challenges) in a single direct comparison. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Though dockless programs offer flexibility, docked bike-share programs provide bicycles for shared use.': Leads with a dockless advantage and ends with a neutral fact true of both program types — no actual disadvantages compared. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
